prompt: If your writing style were a material (wood, brass, cotton, glass, concrete, etc.), which would it be and why?
response:
If my writing style were a material, I would be glass.

Glass is transparent and shimmering, yet solid and durable at the same time. It can take on many shapes and is adaptive to its environment, just like my writing style. I strive to be versatile and fluid, according to the context and audience. Yet, I also aim for clear, sparkling prose that illuminates the ideas beneath.

Also, from the ancient to the artistic, glass has been used in many cultures for their most precious and beautiful objects - windows to gaze through, vessels to hold treasures, and ornaments to admire. These uses mirror my aspiration to craft words that reveal deeper insights, convey meaningful messages and evoke wonder and appreciation in the reader. Thus, by evoking the qualities of glass, I hope to make my writing simultaneously elegant, functional and resonant.
